sections.heading: Overview
sections.paragraph: The April 2026 pharmaceutical intelligence landscape regarding Proteolysis Targeting Chimeras (PROTACs) is defined by strategic infrastructure expansion within the contract development and manufacturing organization sector, specifically highlighted by Ofichem’s consolidation of business units under a single trading name. This Netherlands-based CDMO integrated active pharmaceutical ingredient, drug product, and supply capabilities to support complex modalities, notably expanding non-GMP capabilities for early-phase small-molecule API development at its Uppsala, Sweden facility acquired from Meribel Pharma Solutions in 2025. This site now offers specialized technical expertise in proteolysis targeting chimeras alongside antibody-drug conjugates and oligonucleotides. Concurrently, Ofichem acquired Avivia in 2025 to enhance formulation expertise, reinforcing end-to-end support from early development through commercial manufacturing. Extensive review of broader industry reporting from STAT News and regulatory updates during this period explicitly noted a lack of specific PROTAC pipeline updates or clinical trial data within those datasets. Consequently, current intelligence indicates that PROTAC-specific commercial activity is currently concentrated in manufacturing infrastructure development rather than clinical progression, with stakeholders advised to monitor CDMO capabilities for early-stage degradation modality support amidst a landscape where other therapeutic modalities dominate recent regulatory and policy discussions.

points.text: Ofichem — PROTACs — CDMO Services — Expanded non-GMP capabilities for early-phase small-molecule API development at Uppsala site.
Ofichem: Strategic objective to simplify engagement for customers seeking integrated development and manufacturing partners; associated with PROTAC manufacturing capabilities at Uppsala site.
PROTACs — Proteolysis targeting chimeras — Early-phase small-molecule API development — CDMO capability expansion at Ofichem Uppsala site.
Ofichem acquired site in Uppsala, Sweden from Meribel Pharma Solutions in 2025.
Ofichem acquired Avivia in 2025 to expand pharmaceutical development services with formulation expertise.
